hook: You already know that triangles are the building blocks of geometry — but the world around you is filled with four-sided and many-sided shapes, from floor tiles to stop signs to smartphone screens. So far, you've only scratched the surface of what those shapes can tell you. Get ready to discover the surprisingly rich hidden structure inside quadrilaterals and polygons, and ask yourself: what makes a square more special than a rectangle, or a rectangle more special than a parallelogram?
concept: Every polygon carries a set of angle relationships and symmetry properties that are locked into its very definition — the unifying idea is that shape determines structure, formalized through the concept of 'necessary and sufficient conditions for classification.'

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Interior and Exterior Angles of Polygons
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to calculate the sum of interior angles using $(n-2) \cdot 180°$ and find individual exterior angles for any regular or irregular polygon.

Master the four key properties — opposite sides congruent, opposite angles congruent, consecutive angles supplementary, and diagonals bisecting each other — and apply them to solve for unknown side lengths and angles.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Proving a Quadrilateral is a Parallelogram
topic_roadmap.description: Apply five distinct criteria to construct formal proofs that a given quadrilateral must be a parallelogram, turning properties into powerful two-way tools.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Properties of Special Parallelograms (Rectangles, Rhombi, Squares)
topic_roadmap.description: Distinguish the additional diagonal and angle properties that elevate a parallelogram into a rectangle, rhombus, or square, and use those properties to solve increasingly complex problems.
You will learn to identify and use the unique properties of trapezoids — including the midsegment theorem — and kites to find missing angles, sides, and diagonal relationships.
Apply the distance, midpoint, and slope formulas together to write coordinate proofs that rigorously classify quadrilaterals placed on the coordinate plane.
